questions.text: Which of the following are not polymer?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Proteins are formed by long chains of amino acids, which are their monomers. Hence, they are polymers.
Polysaccharides consist of repeated units of monosaccharides, making them polymers.
Lipids do not have a repetitive monomer structure and are not considered polymers. Their structures are diverse and include components like fatty acids and glycerol.
Nucleic acids are formed from long chains of nucleotides, their monomers, thus classifying them as polymers.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Lipids. Unlike proteins, polysaccharides, and nucleic acids, lipids do not consist of repeating monomer units and therefore are not classified as polymers. Proteins are polymers because they are made up of amino acids linked in chains. Polysaccharides are polymers of monosaccharides, and nucleic acids are polymers of nucleotides, each consisting of a series of repeated structural units.

questions.text: A typical fat molecule is made up of :
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: One glycerol and one fatty acid molecule
options.explanation: A typical fat molecule is made up of three fatty acids and one glycerol molecule.

options.text: Three glycerol and three fatty acid molecules
options.explanation: A typical fat molecule is made up of three fatty acids and one glycerol molecule.

options.text: Three glycerol molecules and one fatty acid molecule
options.explanation: A typical fat molecule is made up of three fatty acids and one glycerol molecule.

options.text: One glycerol and three fatty acid molecules.
options.explanation: Neutral or true fats are triglycerides that are formed by esterification of three molecules of fatty acids with one molecule of trihydric alcohol, glycerol (glycerine or trihydroxy propane).

questions.explanation: Neutral or true fats are triglycerides that are formed by esterification of three molecules of fatty acids with one molecule of trihydric alcohol, glycerol (glycerine or trihydroxy propane).

questions.text: Which one of the following statements is wrong?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This statement is correct. Uracil is one of the four nitrogenous bases found in RNA. It is a pyrimidine base, along with cytosine and thymine. Pyrimidines are a type of organic molecule characterized by a six-membered ring structure.

options.text: Glycine is a sulfur-containing amino acid
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Glycine is the simplest amino acid and does not contain sulfur. It is the only amino acid that is not chiral and has a hydrogen atom as its side chain. Some amino acids, such as cysteine and methionine, do contain sulfur in their structures.

This statement is correct. Sucrose is a disaccharide composed of glucose and fructose units joined by a glycosidic bond. It is commonly known as table sugar and is found in various plant sources, including sugarcane and sugar beets.

This statement is correct. Cellulose is a polysaccharide and one of the main structural components of plant cell walls. It is composed of long chains of glucose units linked by βglycosidic bonds. Cellulose is indigestible by humans but serves as a valuable dietary fiber

questions.explanation: Option A: Uracil is a pyrimidine: This statement is correct. Uracil is one of the four nitrogenous bases found in RNA. It is a pyrimidine base, along with cytosine and thymine. Pyrimidines are a type of organic molecule characterized by a six-membered ring structure.Option B: Glycine is a sulfur-containing amino acid: This statement is incorrect. Glycine is the simplest amino acid and does not contain sulfur. It is the only amino acid that is not chiral and has a hydrogen atom as its side chain. Some amino acids, such as cysteine and methionine, do contain sulfur in their structures.Option C: Sucrose is a disaccharide: This statement is correct. Sucrose is a disaccharide composed of glucose and fructose units joined by a glycosidic bond. It is commonly known as table sugar and is found in various plant sources, including sugarcane and sugar beets.Option D: Cellulose is a polysaccharide: This statement is correct. Cellulose is a polysaccharide and one of the main structural components of plant cell walls. It is composed of long chains of glucose units linked by βglycosidic bonds. Cellulose is indigestible by humans but serves as a valuable dietary fiber

questions.text: The chitinous exoskeleton of arthropods is formed by the polymerisation of _?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: N-acetyl glucosamine is a monosaccharide and a derivative of glucose. It is a key component in the formation of the chitinous exoskeleton of arthropods. Chitin, which is the primary component of the exoskeleton, is a polymer of N-acetyl glucosamine.

Lipoglycans are not directly involved in the formation of the chitinous exoskeleton of arthropods. They are a class of molecules that consist of both lipid (fat) and glycan (sugar) components. They have diverse functions in various biological systems but are not responsible for the structure of arthropod exoskeletons.

Keratin sulfate and chondroitin sulfate are not involved in the formation of the chitinous exoskeleton of arthropods. Keratin sulfate is a component of certain tissues like hair and nails in vertebrates, while chondroitin sulfate is a major component of cartilage.

D glucosamine is a form of glucosamine, which is a naturally occurring amino sugar. While glucosamine plays a role in the synthesis of certain molecules like glycosaminoglycans found in cartilage, it is not directly involved in the polymerization process of chitin to form the chitinous exoskeleton of arthropods.

questions.explanation: Option A: N-acetyl glucosamine is a monosaccharide and a derivative of glucose. It is a key component in the formation of the chitinous exoskeleton of arthropods. Chitin, which is the primary component of the exoskeleton, is a polymer of N-acetyl glucosamine. Option B: Lipoglycans are not directly involved in the formation of the chitinous exoskeleton of arthropods. They are a class of molecules that consist of both lipid (fat) and glycan (sugar) components. They have diverse functions in various biological systems but are not responsible for the structure of arthropod exoskeletons.Option C: Keratin sulfate and chondroitin sulfate are not involved in the formation of the chitinous exoskeleton of arthropods. Keratin sulfate is a component of certain tissues like hair and nails in vertebrates, while chondroitin sulfate is a major component of cartilage. Option D: D glucosamine is a form of glucosamine, which is a naturally occurring amino sugar. While glucosamine plays a role in the synthesis of certain molecules like glycosaminoglycans found in cartilage, it is not directly involved in the polymerization process of chitin to form the chitinous exoskeleton of arthropods.

questions.text: Which one out of A–D given below correctly represents the structural formula of the basic amino acid?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A is Glutamic acid which is an acidic amino acid.
B is the structure of Serine which is a neutral amino acid.
This is an alkanolamine. Not an amino acid.

Basic amino acids have an additional amino group without forming amides thus they are diamino monocarboxylic acids e.g., arginine, lysine, etc. D is the structure of lysine which is a basic amino acid.

questions.explanation: Basic amino acids have an additional amino group without forming amides thus they are diamino monocarboxylic acids e.g., arginine, lysine, etc. D is the structure of lysine which is a basic amino acid.

questions.text: Which one is the most abundant protein in the animal world?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Trypsin is a digestive enzyme that breaks down proteins in the small intestine by hydrolyzing peptide bonds at the C-terminal side of lysine and arginine residues. It is secreted by the pancreas in an inactive form called trypsinogen and is essential for protein digestion, but it also plays roles in inflammation, immune response, and tissue repair, hence it is not abundant.
This is a functional protein present in blood and transports oxygen. Thus, it is abundant in blood not in animal world.

Collagen is an insoluble fibrous protein found extensively in the connective tissue of skin, tendons, and bone. Collagen accounts for over 30% of the total body proteins of mammals, and it is the most abundant animal protein.
Insulin is a hormone released from pancreas. It regulates blood glucose level and is not abundant in animal world.

questions.explanation: Collagen is an insoluble fibrous protein found extensively in the connective tissue of skin, tendons, and bone. Collagen accounts for over 30% of the total body proteins of mammals, and it is the most abundant animal protein.

questions.text: Which one of the following biomolecules is correctly characterized?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Lecithin a phosphorylated glyceride found in the cell membrane.
options.explanation: Lecithin is a triglyceride lipid where one fatty acid is replaced by phosphoric acid, which is linked to an additional nitrogenous group called choline. It is a common membrane lipid. It is an amphipathic phospholipid, having both hydrophilic polar and hydrophobic nonpolar groups. The hydrocarbon chains of two fatty acids function as hydrophobic non­polar tails, whereas the phosphate and choline behave as a hydrophilic polar head group of the molecule.

options.text: Palmitic acid ­an unsaturated fatty acid with 18 carbon atoms.
options.explanation: Palmitic acid is a saturated fatty acid (as it does not possess double bonds in its carbon chain) and contains 16 carbon atoms with formula C 16 H 32 O2.

options.text: Adenylic acid adenosine with a glucose phosphate molecule.
options.explanation: Adenylic acid or adenosine monophosphate is a nucleotide formed by the union of adenine (nitrogenous base), ribose (pentose sugar), and phosphate. It is formed through phosphorylation of nucleoside as phosphate combines with sugar molecule at its 5 carbon atom.

options.text: Alanine amino acid­ contains an amino group and an acidic group anywhere in the molecule.
options.explanation: Amino acids are organic acids with a carboxylic group (–COOH) having an amino group (– NH 2) generally attached to a­ carbon or carbon next to a carboxylic group. Alanine is a nonpolar and neutral amino acid having one methyl group and having an amino group attached to the carbon next to the carboxylic group.

questions.explanation: Lecithin is a triglyceride lipid where one fatty acid is replaced by phosphoric acid, which is linked to an additional nitrogenous group called choline. It is a common membrane lipid. It is an amphipathic phospholipid, having both hydrophilic polar and hydrophobic nonpolar groups. The hydrocarbon chains of two fatty acids function as hydrophobic non­polar tails, whereas the phosphate and choline behave as a hydrophilic polar head group of the molecule. Palmitic acid is a saturated fatty acid (as it does not possess double bonds in its carbon chain) and contains 16 carbon atoms with formula C 16 H 32 O2. Adenylic acid or adenosine monophosphate is a nucleotide formed by the union of adenine (nitrogenous base), ribose (pentose sugar), and phosphate. It is formed through phosphorylation of nucleoside as phosphate combines with sugar molecule at its 5 carbon atom. Amino acids are organic acids with a carboxylic group (–COOH) having an amino group (– NH 2) generally attached to a­ carbon or carbon next to a carboxylic group. Alanine is a nonpolar and neutral amino acid having one methyl group and having an amino group attached to the carbon next to the carboxylic group.

questions.text: Carbohydrates are commonly found as starch in plants storage organs. Which of the following five properties of starch make it useful as a storage material?(1) Easily translocated(2) Chemically non­reactive(3) Easily digested by animals(4) Osmotically inactive(5) Synthesized during photosynthesisThe useful properties are?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: 1st,3rd and 5th properties don't tell about its storage function.Starch is not easily translocated; it is stored where it is made or moved to specific storage organs as a soluble sugar (sucrose) first.While it is digestible by animals, this is a property useful for animals, not the plant itself in terms of storage function within the plant's cells.It is synthesized during photosynthesis, but the process of synthesis is not the property that makes it a good storage material.

1st and 5th properties are irrelevant to its storage function.Starch is not easily translocated; it is stored where it is made or moved to specific storage organs as a soluble sugar (sucrose) first.It is synthesized during photosynthesis, but the process of synthesis is not the property that makes it a good storage material.

3rd property doesn't tell anything about its storage function.While it is digestible by animals, this is a property useful for animals, not the plant itself in terms of storage function within the plant's cells.

Starch is the major storage carbohydrate of plants. In most plant species it is accumulated in the chloroplast of leaves, whereas in storage organs it accumulates in the amyloplast as reserve starch. It is the osmotically inactive form of photosynthetic product and is a hexosan polysaccharide made of many glucose units, so it is chemically non-reactive.

questions.explanation: Starch is the major storage carbohydrate of plants. In most plant species it is accumulated in the chloroplast of leaves, whereas in storage organs it accumulates in the amyloplast as reserve starch. It is the osmotically inactive form of photosynthetic product and is a hexosan polysaccharide made of a large number of glucose units so it is chemically non-reactive.

questions.text: About 98 percent of the mass of every living organism is composed of just six elements including carbon, hydrogen, nitrogen, oxygen and :
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: While carbon is one of the primary elements, sulfur is not among the six major elements that constitute most of the mass of living organisms.
Phosphorus is indeed one of the six essential elements, but calcium is not typically included among the top six that account for 98 percent of mass in living organisms.
Sulfur is considered one of the major elements, but magnesium is classified as a trace element and is not part of the primary six elements.
Both magnesium and sodium are important but are classified as trace elements and do not comprise the bulk of the mass of living organisms.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Phosphorus and Calcium. In the context of biological molecules, the six elements that make up about 98% of the mass of living organisms are carbon (C), hydrogen (H), nitrogen (N), oxygen (O), phosphorus (P), and sulfur (S). Although phosphorus is included, calcium is more of a structural element in bones and is not one of the primary elements constituting the majority of living organism mass.Option A (Carbon and Sulfur) is incorrect because, while carbon is essential, sulfur is not one of the six key elements. Option C (Sulfur and Magnesium) is incorrect as magnesium is a trace element and not one of the primary six. Option D (Magnesium and Sodium) is also incorrect for similar reasons: both are trace elements and do not account for the bulk of the mass of living organisms.

questions.text: Which of the following is the simplest amino acid?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Alanine has one amino group, one carboxylic group and one methyl group attached. It is the second-simplest amino acid, but the simplest is glycine.
It is non-essential amino acid having two amino group and two carboxylic group with methyl group attached.
Glycine is considered the simplest amino acid as it has one amino group, one carboxylic group, and no substituent functional group.
Tryosine is the non-essential amino acid with complex structure.

questions.explanation: Glycine is considered the simplest amino acid as it has one amino group, one carboxylic group, and no substituent functional group.

questions.text: Nucleotides are building blocks of nucleic acids. Each nucleotide is a composite molecule made of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A nucleotide consists of a nitrogenous base, a pentose sugar, and a phosphate group, which together form the essential building blocks of nucleic acids.
Incorrect. This option incorrectly includes a hydroxyl group (OH) instead of a phosphate group. A nucleotide requires a phosphate group, not a hydroxyl group, to form its structure.
Incorrect. This option only mentions nitrogenous bases. A complete nucleotide must also include a pentose sugar and a phosphate group to be structurally complete.
Incorrect. This option is missing the nitrogenous base, which is a crucial component in forming a nucleotide. A nucleotide is incomplete without the base.

questions.explanation: A nucleotide is composed of a nitrogenous base, a pentose sugar, and a phosphate group.

questions.text: The major role of minor elements inside living organisms is to act as?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Minor elements are those which are required in the quantity of less than milligram/gram of dry matter, but they are essential for proper growth and development of an organism e.g., Cl, Mn, B, Zn, Cu, Mo, etc. These elements work as non­protein cofactor in enzymes e.g., Zn, Cu, etc. They also take part in oxidation-reduction reactions, e.g., Cu, with variable valency. Chloride ion enhances the activity of salivary amylase. Zinc is required for the activity of carbonic anhydrase and alcohol dehydrogenase, etc.

options.text: Building blocks of important amino acids
options.explanation: Minor elements do not form building blocks of amino acids. So this option is incorrect because in building amino acids major elements are included.
Hormones are glycoproteins, having sugar attached with proteins. Therefore, minor elements are not constituents of hormones.
Minor elements are not binder of cell structure. Elements like calcium are used as binder of cells which are major elements. So this option is not correct.

questions.explanation: Minor elements are those which are required in the quantity of less than milligram/gram of dry matter, but they are essential for proper growth and development of an organism e.g., Cl, Mn, B, Zn, Cu, Mo, etc. These elements work as non­protein cofactor in enzymes e.g., Zn, Cu, etc. They also take part in oxidation-reduction reactions, e.g., Cu, with variable valency. Chloride ion enhances the activity of salivary amylase. Zinc is required for the activity of carbonic anhydrase and alcohol dehydrogenase, etc.

questions.text: Lipids are insoluble in water because lipid molecules are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Lipids are insoluble because of their hydrophobic property (water-repellent). Hydrophillic means water loving and hydrophilic substances are soluble in water.

Lipid molecules are insoluble or sparingly soluble in water but are freely soluble in organic solvents like ether, alcohol, and benzene. The insolubility of lipids in water is due to the fact that the polar groups they contain are much smaller than their nonpolar portions. The nonpolar chains are long, complex hydrophobic hydrocarbon chains. If shaken in water, lipids often form small droplets or micelles. The complex created is called emulsions. These nonpolar proteins give them water-repellent or hydrophobic properties.
They are insoluble in water because of hydrophobic property. Being neutral has nothing to do with solubility.
Amino acids which are building blocks of proteins act as zwitter ions so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Lipid molecules are insoluble or sparingly soluble in water but are freely soluble in organic solvents like ether, alcohol, and benzene. The insolubility of lipids in water is due to the fact that the polar groups they contain are much smaller than their nonpolar portions. The nonpolar chains are long, complex hydrophobic hydrocarbon chains. If shaken in water, lipids often form small droplets or micelles. The complex created is called emulsions. These nonpolar proteins give them water-repellent or hydrophobic properties.

questions.text: A bus and a car, moving at the same speed, are brought to rest by applying the same retarding force then:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Bus will come to rest in a shorter distance
options.explanation: Option A is incorrect as bus having more mass and hence more inertia will come to rest after the car.

options.text: Car will come to rest in a shorter distance
options.explanation: The distance they move will depend on their deceleration, which in turn would depend on the frictional force exerted by their respective brakes. If both were to experience the same frictional force 'F', then as per F=ma it is evident that the car having the lesser mass will decelerate faster ( greater magnitude of deceleration) and will therefore come to a halt sooner. Conceptually this is due to the effect of inertia as the bus has a greater mass of two vehicles.

options.text: Both will come to rest in the same distance
options.explanation: Option C is incorrect as bus having more mass and hence more inertia will come to rest after the car.
This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The distance they move will depend on their deceleration, which in turn would depend on the frictional force exerted by their respective brakes. If both were to experience the same frictional force 'F', then as per F=ma it is evident that the car having the lesser mass will decelerate faster ( greater magnitude of deceleration) and will therefore come to a halt sooner. Conceptually this is due to the effect of inertia as the bus has a greater mass of two vehicles.

questions.text: 1 MWh is equal to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is not a standard unit. JK does not represent any known energy unit.
This is also not a standard unit for 1 MWh. It's too small.
This is closer, but still not the correct conversion. 1 MWh is much larger than 3.6 MJ.

1 MWh (megawatt-hour) is a unit of energy equal to one million watt-hours (1,000,000 Wh). To convert it to joules (J), you can use the conversion factor that 1 watt-hour is equal to 3,600 joules.This is the correct conversion. 1 MWh is equal to 3.6 gigajoules (GJ).So, the right answer is option D) 3.6 GJ.

questions.explanation: Solution:- Mega watt hour is related with joule as:1MWh =1 x 10^6 x 3600 Ws=3.6 x 10^9 J1MWh = 3.6 GJ

questions.text: Work done is equal to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Work done can be equal to the change in an object's kinetic energy. This occurs when the speed or velocity of an object changes. The formula for kinetic energy is KE = 1/2 * m * v^2, where m is mass and v is velocity.

Work done can also be equal to the change in an object's potential energy. This typically refers to gravitational potential energy, where an object's height above the ground changes. The formula for gravitational potential energy is PE = m * g * h, where m is mass, g is the acceleration due to gravity, and h is the height.

Work done can be equal to the change in elastic potential energy when dealing with objects like springs. When a spring is compressed or stretched, it stores potential energy. The formula for elastic potential energy is PE = 1/2 * k * x^2, where k is the spring constant and x is the displacement from the equilibrium position.

Work done can indeed be equal to the change in any of these forms of energy (kinetic, gravitational potential, or elastic potential) depending on the situation. In many cases, multiple energy forms may be involved in a single process.

questions.explanation: Solution:- According to work-energy principle“Work done on a body is equal to change in its K.E or change in its P.E or change in both energies.”i.e W=Δ K.E or ΔP .E or both.

questions.text: Which of the following is unit of P.E:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is a unit of energy commonly used in Particle Physics and Atomic Physics. It represents the energy gained or lost by an electron, when it moves through an electric potential difference of one volt.
This is a unit of energy used in nutrition and thermodynamics. It represents the amount of energy required to raise the temperature of one gram of water by one degree Celsius.

The joule is the standard International System of Units (SI) unit of energy. It is defined as the energy transferred when a force of one newton is applied over a distance of one meter. It is a fundamental unit used in various scientific and engineering contexts.
The correct answer to your question is "All of these," as all of them are valid units of energy, but they are used in different scientific disciplines.

questions.explanation: Solution:- The different units of energy and their relation with SI-unit is as following:1 kWh = 3.6 MJ1 eV = 1.6 × 10-19 J1 calorie = 4.18 J1 erg = 10-7 J

questions.text: Slope of energy-time graph is equal to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The slope of an energy-time graph is not equal to acceleration. Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity with respect to time and is unrelated to energy.
The slope of an energy-time graph is also not equal to momentum. Momentum is the product of an object's mass and its velocity and is not directly related to the energy-time relationship.
Power is the rate at which energy is transferred or converted per unit of time. The slope of an energy-time graph can indeed represent power, as it indicates how fast energy is changing with time.

Work is the energy transferred to or from an object when a force is applied over a distance. The slope of an energy-time graph is not directly equal to work but can indirectly relate to it if the energy change over time is due to work being done.

questions.explanation: Slope = Δ Energy/Δtime = Power

questions.text: For what angle between F and d work reduces to half of its maximum value?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If θ is 60 degrees, cos(60°) = 0.5. So, it reduces the work to half its maximum value.
If θ is 30 degrees, cos(30°) = √3/2 ≈ 0.866. This angle results in less than half of the maximum work.
If θ is 45 degrees, cos(45°) = 1/√2 ≈ 0.707. This angle also results in less than half of the maximum work.
If θ is 90 degrees, cos(90°) = 0. This angle results in no work being done, so it's not what we're looking for.

questions.explanation: W=FxdcosθW=Fxdcos(60°)W=1/2 Fxd

questions.text: If the K.E of a particle is quadrupled, then its momentum will:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Momentum is directly related to the square root of K.E, so it cannot remain the same if K.E changes.
Momentum =√2mK.E=√2m4K.E=2 √2mK.ESo, momentum doubles.
Only possible if momentum squared is directly proportional to K.E — but it’s the square root relationship here.
Incorrect, because quadrupling energy increases momentum.

questions.explanation: p=√2m(K. E.)=√2m4K.E=2 √2mK.ESo, momentum doubles.

questions.text: Which one is not a unit of power?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Commercial unit of power. So this is incorrect.
It is the unit of energy. So this option is correct.
It is the SI unit of power. So this option is incorrect according to unit of power.
K is the prefix for kilo and w is watt, it is also a unit of power. This option is also incorrect.

questions.explanation: 1 horse power = 746 W1 kilo watt = 1000 W1 electron volt = 1.6 × 10-19 JThus the correct option is electron volt.

questions.text: 1 horse power is equal to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Joule is the unit of energy, not power, whose unit is horse power.
It is 746 watt and there is no need of prefixes.
1 horse power=746 watt
No need of prefixes. So it is incorrect.

questions.explanation: 1 horse power = 746 W

questions.text: Which one of the following is bigger unit of work?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The watt-hour is a unit of energy commonly used to measure electrical work. 10 watt-hours is a smaller unit of work compared to kilowatt-hour (kWh). One kilowatt-hour is equal to 1,000 watt-hours, so 10 watt-hours is significantly smaller.10 Wh = 10×3600 Ws = 3.6×104 J

The electron volt (eV) is a unit of energy commonly used in atomic and particle physics. The electron volt is a very small unit of energy. 10 electron volts is a relatively small amount of work compared to the other options provided.10 eV = 10×1.6×10-19 J = 1.6×10-18 J.

As mentioned before, the electron volt (eV) is a unit of energy. It is a small unit of energy commonly used in atomic and particle physics, particularly when dealing with individual particles or energy levels of atoms. While it is a useful unit in its specific context, it is smaller than the other options provided.

The kilowatt-hour (kWh) is a unit of energy commonly used to measure electrical work on a larger scale. It represents the amount of energy consumed or produced by a device with a power of 1 kilowatt (1,000 watts) over a period of 1 hour. The kilowatt-hour is a larger unit of work compared to the other options. It is the largest unit of work among the given options.1 kWh = 3.6×106 J.

questions.explanation: 10 Wh = 10×3600 Ws = 3.6 ×104 J10 eV = 10×1.6×10-19 J = 1.6×10-18 J1 kWh = 3.6×106 JHence, the correct option for the bigger unit of work is kilowatt-hour (kWh), as it represents a larger amount of energy compared to the other options.

questions.text: A force of 10 N acts on the body and body moves 10 m distance perpendicular to it. Work done by the force on the body is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect, as upon calculating W=FDcos(θ), 10 is not the answer obtained.
100 joules is incorrect because the work done is not equal to the product of the force and the distance traveled. It is the product of the force, displacement, and cosine of the angle between them.

Correct, as the force of 10 N acts on the body, and the body moves a distance of 10 m perpendicular to the force. Since the force and displacement are perpendicular (θ = 90 degrees), the angle between them is such that cos(θ) = cos(90) = 0. Therefore, the work done is W = F x d x cos(θ) = 10 x 10 x 0 = 0 joules.
Incorrect because the work done is finite and depends on the force and displacement.

questions.explanation: Work (W) is defined as the product of force (F) and displacement (d) in the direction of the force. Mathematically, W = F x d x cos(θ), where θ is the angle between the force vector and the displacement vector.W = F x d x cos(θ) = (10)(10) cos90 = 0

questions.text: Protons, electrons, neutrons, and particles have the same momentum. Which of them has the highest K.E?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Protons have a larger mass than electrons, neutrons, and alpha particles. Therefore, protons have higher kinetic energy than electrons but lower kinetic energy than alpha particles.

The kinetic energy (K.E.) of an object is given by the equation: K.E. = (1/2) x m x v2 where m is the mass and v is the velocity of the object. Given that momentum is the same for all three particles, and momentum is given by the product of mass and velocity, we can conclude that the velocities of protons, electrons, and neutrons must be different. Since kinetic energy depends on the mass and the square of the velocity, the particle with the highest kinetic energy will be the one with the highest velocity. Therefore, the electron, which has the smallest mass among the three particles, will have the highest kinetic energy. The higher velocity of the electron compensates for its smaller mass, resulting in greater kinetic energy compared to protons and neutrons.
Neutrons have a mass similar to protons but are not as heavy as alpha particles. Hence, neutrons have higher kinetic energy than electrons but lower kinetic energy than alpha particles.
Alpha particles consist of two protons and two neutrons, giving them the highest mass among the given options.

questions.explanation: The momentum of a particle is given by the product of its mass and velocity. Since both protons and electrons have the same momentum, it means they must have different velocities due to their different masses.The kinetic energy (K.E) of a particle is given by the equation K.E = (1/2) mv^2, where m is the mass of the particle and v is its velocity.Comparing protons, electrons, and neutrons:Protons have a relatively larger mass compared to electrons and neutrons.Neutrons have a slightly larger mass than protons.Electrons have a much smaller mass compared to protons and neutrons.Given that momentum is the same for all three particles, and momentum is given by the product of mass and velocity, we can conclude that the velocities of protons, electrons, and neutrons must be different.Since kinetic energy depends on the mass and the square of the velocity, the particle with the highest kinetic energy will be the one with the highest velocity.Therefore, the electron, which has the smallest mass among the three particles, will have the highest kinetic energy. The higher velocity of the electron compensates for its smaller mass, resulting in a greater kinetic energy compared to protons and neutrons.

questions.text: Precipitation occurs when the product of ionic concentrations is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ksp (solubility product constant), is an equilibrium constant for the quantitative measure of solubility of solids in solution. It is the product of concentrations of ions of a sparingly soluble salt, at equilibrium, raised to the power of their coefficients in the balanced chemical equation of dissociation. The higher the Ksp, the more soluble the salt is. If Q, the reaction quotient (ionic product) is greater than the Ksp, the excess will be precipitated. Therefore, option A is the correct answer.
Option B is incorrect because if Q is less than Ksp, all the salt will be dissolved in solution completely, without any precipitation.
Option C is incorrect because if Q is equal to Ksp, the salt will be at equilibrium with its ions and there will be no precipitation.

Option D is incorrect because the Ksp of different solutes varies and whether or not it will dissolve completely or precipitate depends upon its value of Ksp. Q being equal to unity does not explain anything.

questions.explanation: Precipitation occurs when the product of ionic concentrations exceeds the solubility product constant (Ksp) for the particular ionic compound in question. The solubility product constant is a measure of the equilibrium solubility of a compound in a solution. Mathematically, the condition for precipitation is expressed as: [Product of ion concentrations] > Ksp The "product of ion concentrations" refers to the product of the molar concentrations (in mol/L) of the ions involved in the compound. It is important to note that only the ionic species that are involved in the compound and are present in the solution are considered for this product. When the product of ion concentrations exceeds the Ksp value, the ionic compound becomes supersaturated and precipitation occurs, leading to the formation of a solid precipitate. Conversely, if the product of ion concentrations is below the Ksp value, the system remains unsaturated, and no precipitation occurs. Ksp (solubility product constant), is an equilibrium constant for the quantitative measure of solubility of solids in solution. It is the product of concentrations of ions of a sparingly soluble salt, at equilibrium, raised to the power of their coefficients in the balanced chemical equation of dissociation. The higher the Ksp, the more soluble the salt is. If Q, the reaction quotient (ionic product) is greater than the Ksp, the excess will be precipitated. Therefore, option A is the correct answer.

questions.text: Le Chatelier Principle is about:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Reaction mixture is the combination of reactants and products in a chemical reaction. It is the system that is at equilibrium.
Reactants are the substances that are present at the start of a chemical reaction. They are converted into products by the reaction.

Le Chatelier's Principle states that: when a system at equilibrium is subjected to an external stress, the system will adjust in such a way as to partially counteract the effect of the stress and restore equilibrium. The principle applies to changes in temperature, pressure, and concentration. By focusing on the equilibrium mixture, it emphasizes that the system will respond to any changes in the concentrations of reactants or products by shifting the equilibrium position to minimize the effect of the change and maintain equilibrium.
Products are the substances that are formed by a chemical reaction. They are produced from the reactants.

questions.explanation: Le Chatelier’s Principle describes how an equilibrium system responds to changes in temperature, pressure, or concentration. It predicts the direction in which the equilibrium will shift to counter the applied change. Therefore, it is specifically about the behavior of an equilibrium mixture.

questions.text: For the reaction: N2 + 3H2 ⇌ 2NH3 .The production of NH3 will be favored at:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: To determine the favored conditions, you can apply Le Chatelier's Principle. Increasing pressure favors the side with fewer moles of gas. Since there are fewer moles on the right (2 moles of NH3) compared to the left (4 moles of N2 and 6 moles of H2), higher pressure would favor the production of NH3.

To determine the favored conditions, you can apply Le Chatelier's Principle. Increasing pressure favors the side with fewer moles of gas. Since there are fewer moles on the right (2 moles of NH3) compared to the left (4 moles of N2 and 6 moles of H2), higher pressure would favor the production of NH3. There this option is not correct.

To determine the favored conditions, you can apply Le Chatelier's Principle. Increasing pressure favors the side with fewer moles of gas. Since there are fewer moles on the right (2 moles of NH3) compared to the left (4 moles of N2 and 6 moles of H2), higher pressure would favor the production of NH3. Therefore this option is not correct.

To determine the favored conditions, you can apply Le Chatelier's Principle. Increasing pressure favors the side with fewer moles of gas. Since there are fewer moles on the right (2 moles of NH3) compared to the left (4 moles of N2 and 6 moles of H2), higher pressure would favor the production of NH3.

To determine the favored conditions, you can apply Le Chatelier's Principle. Increasing pressure favors the side with fewer moles of gas. Since there are fewer moles on the right (2 moles of NH3) compared to the left (4 moles of N2 and 6 moles of H2), higher pressure would favor the production of NH3. Therefore this option is not correct.

questions.explanation: The forward reaction forms fewer moles of gas (4 → 2), so high pressure shifts equilibrium toward NH₃. A catalyst increases the reaction rate but does not change equilibrium position. Therefore, maximum ammonia production is achieved using high pressure along with a catalyst.

questions.text: Which factor does affect the equilibrium constant Kc for a reaction?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Changing the temperature shifts the equilibrium position based on whether the reaction is endothermic (absorbs heat) or exothermic (releases heat), according to Le Chatelier's Principle.3 Since the ratio of products to reactants changes, the value of K must also change.
B is incorrect because pressure changes shift equilibrium position, not the Kc value, which depends only on temperature for a given reaction.
C is incorrect because catalysts speed up reaching equilibrium but do not alter the Kc value, which is temperature‑dependent only in chemistry.
D is incorrect because initial concentrations determine the reaction quotient, not the Kc, which is a fixed temperature‑dependent constant for each reaction.

questions.explanation: Changing the temperature shifts the equilibrium position based on whether the reaction is endothermic (absorbs heat) or exothermic (releases heat), according to Le Chatelier's Principle.3 Since the ratio of products to reactants changes, the value of K must also change.

questions.text: At equilibrium, which of the following reactions is not affected by pressure?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This reaction involves the formation of nitrogen monoxide (NO) from nitrogen gas (N2) and oxygen gas (O2). Since the stoichiometric coefficients of all the gases in the balanced equation are equal, a change in pressure will not affect the equilibrium position. The correct option is A.

In this reaction, phosphorus pentachloride (PCl5) decomposes into phosphorus trichloride (PCl3) and chlorine gas (Cl2). The balanced equation contains gaseous reactants, so changes in pressure will affect the equilibrium position.

This reaction involves the equilibrium between nitrogen dioxide (NO2) and dinitrogen tetroxide (N2O4). The stoichiometric coefficients are not equal for the reactants and products, so changes in pressure will affect the equilibrium position. An increase in pressure will favor the formation of N2O4, while a decrease in pressure will favor the formation of NO2.

This reaction represents the equilibrium between thionyl chloride (SOCl2), sulfur dioxide (SO2), and chlorine gas (Cl2). The stoichiometric coefficients are not equal for the reactants and products, so changes in pressure will affect the equilibrium position.

questions.explanation: Pressure affects only reactions where the number of gas moles changes. In option A, reactants have 1 mole of gas total (0.5 + 0.5), and the product also has 1 mole. Since gas moles are equal on both sides, pressure has no effect on this equilibrium.

questions.text: In a reaction:A+B < - > 2C When equilibrium was attained, the concentration was [A] = [B] = 4 moles/dm3[C]= 6 moles/dm3The equilibrium constant Kc of this reaction is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Double-check your calculation using the formula: Kc = [C]² / [A][B].
This is correct. Calculate Kc as: Kc = [C]² / [A][B] = 6² / 4 x 4 = 36 / 16 = 2.25.
This option is incorrect. Review the stoichiometry and ensure correct substitution of concentrations.
This option is incorrect. Verify the concentration values and apply the equilibrium expression accurately.
This option is incorrect. The result does not align with the equilibrium expression based on the balanced equation.

questions.explanation: The equilibrium constant Kc is calculated using the expression: Kc = [C]² / [A][B]. For the reaction A + B < - > 2C, substitute the equilibrium concentrations: [C] = 6 moles/dm3, [A] = [B] = 4 moles/dm3. Thus, Kc = (6)² / (4)(4) = 36 / 16 = 2.25, confirming Option B as correct. Options A, C, D, and E result from errors in calculation or misinterpretation of the equilibrium constant formula.

questions.text: The presence of common ions --------- the solubility of a slightly soluble ionic compound
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The presence of a common ion decreases the solubility of a slightly soluble ionic compound. This phenomenon is known as the common ion effect.Here's how it works:Slightly soluble ionic compounds dissociate partially in water, forming ions in solution.When we add another soluble ionic compound that shares a common ion with the slightly soluble compound, the equilibrium between the solid and dissolved state shifts towards the solid phase.This happens because the addition of the common ion increases the concentration of that specific ion in the solution. According to Le Chatelier's principle, the equilibrium tries to counteract this change by reducing the amount of dissolved ions, hence decreasing the solubility of the slightly soluble compound
Increases: As explained above, the common ion effect actually decreases the solubility, not increases it.

Neither decrease nor increase: The common ion effect has a definite impact on solubility, either decreasing or increasing it depending on the specific system. In the case of slightly soluble ionic compounds, it always decreases the solubility.
None: This is not a valid option as the common ion effect has a real and well-understood influence on the solubility of slightly soluble ionic compounds

questions.explanation: The presence of a common ion decreases the solubility of a slightly soluble ionic compound. This phenomenon is known as the common ion effect.Here's how it works:Slightly soluble ionic compounds dissociate partially in water, forming ions in solution.When we add another soluble ionic compound that shares a common ion with the slightly soluble compound, the equilibrium between the solid and dissolved state shifts towards the solid phase.This happens because the addition of the common ion increases the concentration of that specific ion in the solution. According to Le Chatelier's principle, the equilibrium tries to counteract this change by reducing the amount of dissolved ions, hence decreasing the solubility of the slightly soluble compound

questions.text: When forward reaction and reverse reaction occur at the same time it is called
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Forward equilibrium - This is incorrect. "Forward equilibrium" doesn't accurately describe the concept. Equilibrium implies a balance between the forward and reverse reactions.
Reverse equilibrium - This is incorrect. Similar to option (a), "Reverse equilibrium" is not a standard term. Equilibrium refers to the balance between the forward and reverse reactions

When the forward reaction and the reverse reaction occur at the same time and at the same rate, the system is in a state of dynamic equilibrium. At chemical equilibrium, the concentrations of reactants and products remain constant over time, but both reactions continue to occur. The rates of the forward and reverse reactions are equal, leading to no net change in the concentrations of the reactants and products.

None of the above - This is incorrect. The correct term for the situation described is "chemical equilibrium." It is a well-established concept in chemistry when both the forward and reverse reactions are occurring simultaneously at the same rate

questions.explanation: When the forward reaction and the reverse reaction occur at the same time and at the same rate, the system is in a state of dynamic equilibrium. At chemical equilibrium, the concentrations of reactants and products remain constant over time, but both reactions continue to occur. The rates of the forward and reverse reactions are equal, leading to no net change in the concentrations of the reactants and products.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a buffer solution?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Acetic acid {CH3COOH} is a weak acid. Buffer solutions are prepared by mixing weak acid and salt of it with strong base, which is the case here, so this is a buffer solution.
Carbonic acid {H2CO3} is a weak acid. Buffer solutions are prepared by mixing weak acid and salt of it with strong base, which is the case here, so this is a buffer solution.

An acidic buffer solution is prepared by mixing weak acid and salt of it with a strong base. So HCl + NaCl cannot form an acidic buffer solution because HCl is a strong acid and it does not fulfil the condition of an acidic buffer solution.
Phosphoric (V) oxide {H3PO4} is a weak acid. Buffer solutions are prepared by mixing weak acid and salt of it with strong base, which is the case here, so this is a buffer solution.

questions.explanation: An acidic buffer solution is prepared by mixing weak acid and salt of it with a strong base. So HCl + NaCl cannot form an acidic buffer solution because HCl is a strong acid and it does not fulfil the condition of an acidic buffer solution.

questions.text: For the homogeneous reaction, 4NH₃ + 5O₂ ⇌ 4NO + 6H₂O, the units of the equilibrium constant (K꜀) are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The units for K are determined by (concentration) , where Δn is the change in moles of gas (products - reactants). Here, Δn = (4+6) - (4+5) = 1. Therefore, the unit is (concentration)¹, or mol·dm⁻³.

questions.explanation: The units for K are determined by (concentration) , where Δn is the change in moles of gas (products - reactants). Here, Δn = (4+6) - (4+5) = 1. Therefore, the unit is (concentration)¹, or mol·dm⁻³.

questions.text: Chemical equilibrium is not associated with:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Chemical equilibrium is a macroscopic property, meaning that it can be observed on a large scale. For example, if you have a closed container with a mixture of reactants and products, you will be able to see that the amount of reactants and products does not change over time, even though the reaction is still occurring.

options.text: At equilibrium, the amount of reactants = the amount of products
options.explanation: At equilibrium, the amount of reactants may not necessarily be equal to the amount of products. However, at equilibrium the rate of forward reaction = rate of reverse reaction.

options.text: It is established in closed system
options.explanation: True. Equilibrium can only be reached in a closed system, so that reactants and products cannot escape, and the reaction can proceed in both directions.

options.text: It can establish from either side of reactant or product
options.explanation: True. Equilibrium can be approached starting with only reactants, only products, or a mixture. Eventually, the same equilibrium concentrations are reached.

questions.explanation: At equilibrium, the rate of the forward reaction equals the rate of the backward reaction, but the amounts of reactants and products are generally not equal.For example, in:N2+3H2⟷2NH3​Even at equilibrium, the concentrations of N2,H2, and NH3NH​ are not equal.So option B is incorrect statement

questions.text: The addition of a catalyst to a chemical reaction changes:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The enthalpy of a reaction is the heat released or absorbed during the reaction. The addition of a catalyst does not change the enthalpy of a reaction.
The entropy of a reaction is the measure of disorder in a system. The addition of a catalyst does not change the entropy of a reaction.
The catalyst decreases the activation energy so more molecules can react and reaction rate increases.
ΔG depends on the free energy difference between reactants and products, which a catalyst does not change.

questions.explanation: A catalyst provides an alternative reaction pathway with lower activation energy.This increases the reaction rate without affecting ΔH, ΔS, or ΔG.Option C is correct

questions.text: For a particular reversible reaction If [Products]/[Reactants]>Kc It means that:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: More products are needed to attain equillbrium
options.explanation: If [Products]/[Reactants] is greater than Kc, it indicates that the reaction has not yet reached equilibrium. The reaction may continue in the forward direction to form more products, but it does not necessarily mean it will reach equilibrium.

options.text: There are more reactants in the system
options.explanation: There are more reactants in the system: This option is not correct. If [Products]/[Reactants] is greater than Kc, it suggests that the concentration of products is higher than that of reactants, not the other way around.

options.text: More Reactants are needed to attain equillbrium
options.explanation: More reactants are needed to attain equilibrium: This option is correct. If [Products]/[Reactants] is greater than Kc, it implies that the reaction has not yet reached equilibrium, and more reactants are needed to establish a balance between the forward and reverse reactions.

options.text: The reaction will move forward to attain equilibrium
options.explanation: The reaction will move forward to attain equilibrium: This option is not entirely correct. If [Products]/[Reactants] is greater than Kc, it indicates that the reaction has not yet reached equilibrium. The reaction may continue in the forward direction to form more products, but it does not necessarily mean it will reach equilibrium.

questions.explanation: If [Products]/[Reactants]>Kc then it means there are more products than needed for the equilibrium value, hence more Reactants are needed to attain equillbrium

questions.text: Which statement describes the electric potential difference between two points in the electric field of charge Q?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: The difference of electric field between the points per unit charge.
options.explanation: This statement describes the electric field, not the electric potential difference. Electric field (E) is the force experienced by a unit positive charge at a point in space. Electric potential difference, on the other hand, refers to the difference in electric potential (voltage) between two points in an electric field. While electric field is measured in units of force per unit charge (N/C), electric potential difference is measured in volts (V).

options.text: The ratio of the power dissipated between the points to the mass of charge.
options.explanation: This statement describes neither electric field nor electric potential difference accurately. Power is the rate at which work is done or energy is transferred, typically measured in watts (W). The concept of power dissipation may relate to energy loss in a circuit, but it doesn't directly define electric potential difference.

options.text: The work done in moving a test charge between points divided by the magnitude of the test charge.
options.explanation: This statement correctly defines electric potential difference. Electric potential difference (V) between two points is the work done per unit charge (Q) in moving a test charge from one point to the other. Mathematically, it is expressed as V=W/Q​, where V is the potential difference, W is the work done, and Q is the magnitude of the test charge.

options.text: The force required to move a unit positive charge between the points per unit charge.
options.explanation: This statement describes electric field strength, not electric potential difference. Electric field strength (E) is the force experienced by a unit positive charge at a point in an electric field. It's measured in newtons per coulomb (N/C). While electric potential difference is related to electric field, they represent different aspects of the electric field.

questions.explanation: This question is simply revising how the electric potential is defined as it is work done per unit charge in moving that charge from one place to the other in the electric field.All other options are not the definitions of electric potential.
hints: describes the electric potential difference between two points in the electric field of charge Q

questions.text: If a dielectric material is placed between two plates of a capacitor, the net capacitance of the capacitor:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When a dielectric material is placed between two plates of a capacitor, the presence of the dielectric reduces the electric field between the plates. This is because the polarized molecules in the dielectric material align themselves with the electric field, weakening it. As a result, the capacitance of the capacitor increases. Therefore, option (a) is not correct.

○The strength of the electric field is reduced due to the presence of dielectric. If the total charge on the plates is kept constant, then the potential difference is reduced across the capacitor plates. In this way, dielectric increases the capacitance of the capacitor.So, option A,C,D is wrong .

If a dielectric material is placed between the plates of a capacitor and the separation distance between the plates remains the same, the capacitance will increase due to the dielectric constant of the material. Therefore, the net capacitance of the capacitor does not remain constant. Hence, option (c) is not correct.

If the dielectric material completely fills the space between the plates of a capacitor, the electric field between the plates is essentially eliminated. In this case, the capacitance of the capacitor becomes zero because there is no potential difference between the plates. However, in most practical scenarios, the dielectric material is not in direct contact with the plates but is inserted between them. Therefore, the option (d) is not correct

questions.explanation: ○The strength of the electric field is reduced due to the presence of dielectric. If the total charge on the plates is kept constant, then the potential difference is reduced across the capacitor plates. In this way, dielectric increases the capacitance of the capacitor.So, option A,C,D is wrong .
hints: dielectric material is placed between two plates of a capacitor

questions.text: In what form is the energy stored in a charged capacitor?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Energy stored in a charged capacitor is not in the form of gravitational energy. Gravitational energy is associated with the gravitational field and the position of objects relative to each other in a gravitational field, whereas a capacitor stores energy in an electric field.

This option is partially correct. The energy stored in a charged capacitor is indeed related to electrical potential. When a capacitor is charged, work is done to move charges from one plate to the other, creating a potential difference between the plates. This potential difference, also known as voltage, represents stored electrical energy in the capacitor.

Energy stored in a charged capacitor is not in the form of a magnetic field. Capacitors primarily store energy in an electric field between their plates. While there might be small induced magnetic fields around the conductors carrying current during charging and discharging processes, the predominant energy storage mechanism in a capacitor is electric, not

This option is somewhat related to option b). The energy stored in a capacitor is indeed related to potential, specifically electrical potential or voltage. The potential difference between the plates of the capacitor, caused by the accumulation of charges on the plates, represents the stored energy.

questions.explanation: Energy is stored in a form of electrostatic field between plates of capacitor. This energy can be expressed in the form of work done by the battery.

questions.text: A particle carrying charge of 2e falls through a potential difference of 3.0 V. Calculate energy acquired by it.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Correct. Using the formula Energy = qV, where q = 2e and V = 3.0 V, we get Energy = 6eV. Converting to joules, Energy = 6 × 1.6 × 10-19 J = 9.6 × 10-19 J.
Incorrect. This value does not match the product of 6eV converted to joules.
Incorrect. This value is not obtained from the correct calculation of charge and potential difference.
Incorrect. This value exceeds the correct energy calculation for the given variables.
Incorrect. This value is not calculated from the formula Energy = qV with the given parameters.

questions.explanation: To find the energy acquired by the particle, use the formula: Energy = qV, where q is the charge and V is the potential difference. The charge of the particle is 2e, where e is the elementary charge, approximately 1.6 × 10-19 C. The potential difference is given as 3.0 V. Thus, Energy = 2e × 3 V = 6eV. Converting eV to joules gives: Energy = 6 × 1.6 × 10-19 J = 9.6 × 10-19 J.Options B, C, D, and E are incorrect because they do not follow the correct calculation using the formula and given values.

questions.text: The total outward flux over any closed hypothetical surface called _ is equal to the total charge enclosed divided by ε irrespective of how the charge is distributed.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The term 'Coulomb surface' is not used in physics. Coulomb relates to the unit of electric charge and does not define a surface relevant to Gauss's law.
Correct! A Gaussian surface is an imaginary closed surface used in Gauss's law to calculate the electric flux, facilitating the computation by aligning with the symmetry of the charge distribution.
'Ohm' refers to electrical resistance and is unrelated to defining surfaces in the context of electric flux or Gauss's law.
While 'Faraday' is a well-known name in electromagnetism, it is not used to describe surfaces relevant to Gauss's law for electric flux.

questions.explanation: Gauss's law states that the total electric flux through a closed surface, also known as a Gaussian surface, is equal to the total charge enclosed by the surface divided by the permittivity of the medium (ε). This law helps in calculating electric fields around charge distributions with symmetry. The other terms, 'Coulomb surface', 'Ohm surface', and 'Faraday surface', do not apply to this context. Coulomb is related to charge, Ohm to resistance, and Faraday to electromagnetic induction, none of which define such a surface for Gauss's law.

questions.text: When a charge Q on a capacitor is doubled, then energy stored U will be:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Doubling the charge does not simply double the energy since energy is proportional to the square of the charge.
There is no linear relationship that leads to tripling the energy when the charge is doubled.
Decreasing energy when the charge is doubled contradicts the principle that energy is proportional to the square of the charge.
The energy stored in a capacitor is proportional to the square of the charge. Therefore, when the charge is doubled, the energy increases by a factor of four.

questions.explanation: The energy stored in a capacitor (U) is given by the formula:U = (1/2) * C * V^2where C is the capacitance of the capacitor and V is the voltage across the capacitor.When a charge (Q) on a capacitor is doubled, the voltage (V) across the capacitor remains the same if the capacitance (C) is constant. This is because the capacitance of a capacitor is defined as the ratio of charge to voltage, and if the charge is doubled while the voltage remains the same, the capacitance remains unchanged.So, in this case, if Q is doubled, the voltage V remains the same. Substituting this into the formula for energy stored in a capacitor:U = (1/2) * Q^2/CSince V remains constant, the energy stored U is proportional to the square of the charge Q. Thus, when the charge on a capacitor is doubled, the energy stored in the capacitor will be quadrupled (i.e., multiplied by a factor of 4).

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because "he" is a subject pronoun, not an object pronoun. The sentence requires an object pronoun to match the structure of "I have never seen."

"Be charged" is the verb phrase in question. It should be in the form "can be charged" to convey the passive voice correctly. This is the correct option.
"Times" is a noun that indicates the frequency or number of occurrences. In this sentence, it correctly describes how many times the batteries can be charged (hundreds of times).
